raw.objective: “to build a population pharmacokinetic (PPK) model of VPA in Chinese children with epilepsy and to predict serum concentrations for new patients using a Bayesian approach.”
raw.covariates: Tested: SEX, AGE, WT, HT, Creatinine levels and CL. Included: -
raw.observations: Interaction between other AED’s was noted in study, but not included in model. They observed that PHE, PHT, CBZ induced metabolism of VPA VPA serum concentrations can be predicted by this Bayesian approach model. F is fixed in model; No apparent racial variability discovered in model between Occidental and Chinese. .
raw.dose: Steady state, oral doses in patients
raw.structure: one-compartment model with first-order absorption and elimination
raw.equations: KA = theta1*exp(eta1) V = theta2*WT*exp(eta2) Kel = theta3*exp(eta3) CL = Kel*V #no error model reported Cobs=Cipred
raw.parameter_values: theta1= 2.522 theta2= 0.329 theta3= 0.0438 theta4= -0.03 theta5= 1.03 theta6= 0.90 omega1=0.4559 omega2=0.4694 omega3=0.5886
raw.validation: Yes; with a validation cohort, N=100

structural_model.description: one-compartment model with first-order absorption and elimination

omega.conversion: Appendix NONMEM-scale OMEGA value retained verbatim; source-paper verification pending
Appendix NONMEM-scale OMEGA value retained verbatim; source-paper verification pending
Appendix NONMEM-scale OMEGA value retained verbatim; source-paper verification pending

extraction_target.residual_error: KA = theta1*exp(eta1) V = theta2*WT*exp(eta2) Kel = theta3*exp(eta3) CL = Kel*V #no error model reported Cobs=Cipred
extraction_target.notes: Appendix-derived silver reference; compare with the publication before promotion to a gold tier.

study.dose: Steady state, oral doses in patients
study.objective: “to build a population pharmacokinetic (PPK) model of VPA in Chinese children with epilepsy and to predict serum concentrations for new patients using a Bayesian approach.”

covariates.raw: Tested: SEX, AGE, WT, HT, Creatinine levels and CL. Included: -

validation.raw: Yes; with a validation cohort, N=100

normalization.omega: Values labelled OMEGA in the appendix are retained as NONMEM variances; no CV reconversion is applied.
normalization.eta: ETA distribution is inferred only when the appendix equation explicitly uses exp(ETA) or additive ETA.
normalization.source_text: The appendix row is preserved verbatim in raw; normalized fields never replace it.
